clinical_criteria: 410.* Code must occur on two successive days for MI definition
For all text mentions of events, use only the first text mention as a qualifying event
Follow up requires confirmation of discharge summary, history and physical, past medical history or clinic visit or clinic note, or any clinical report
Follow up may be defined as a problem list consisting of a non-zero medication section or non-zero problem section
algorithm_summary: This phenotype identifies clopidogrel poor metabolizers based on adverse cardiovascular events after clopidogrel use. Cases are defined as patients who experience MI, stroke, or need for revascularization following stent placement and clopidogrel therapy. The algorithm uses CPT codes to identify stent placement (92980, 92981) and revascularization procedures (coronary artery bypass codes 33510-33536 series), ICD-9 codes for MI (410.* on two successive days) and stroke (433.* and 434.* series), plus text mentions of relevant terms. Controls require adequate follow-up (1-2 years) without adverse events. Both versions of the phenotype (1-year and 2-year follow-up) use identical code sets but differ in follow-up duration requirements.
